title: bumelia americana subsp. neglecta
intro: Bumelia americana subsp. neglecta, also known as the Desert Bumelia, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Desert Bumelia has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the Desert Bumelia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
